meta.title: Wilvardo Ramírez · Product director, advisor and developer
meta.description: A decade leading and building software development and product design. A technical introduction: capabilities, standards and ways to work together.

hero.sub: A decade building and leading software product and development in real production. This is my technical introduction.

items: I REVIEW EVERY CHANGE BEFORE EVERY DEPLOY

about.headline: For a decade I've built and led software teams and product.
about.body1: I'm analytical and a researcher by nature: before deciding, I gather as much information as possible. I lead software development and product design in real production, where quality goes through me; I review every change before every deploy.
about.body2: I hold a critical, responsible stance on AI: I use it as an accelerator with judgment, never as a substitute for thinking. The code I deliver is understood, justified and maintainable.
stats: Years building software in real production.
Of all changes reviewed before every deploy.

caps.headline: What I can actually do.
caps.sub: Not a list of buzzwords. It's the inventory of what I can diagnose, build and lead today.

groups.title: Languages & stack
groups.desc: The stack I ship product with every day.

groups.title: Architecture & engineering
groups.desc: Code that scales and stays maintainable, always caring for UX and UI.

groups.title: Product, process & leadership
groups.desc: From a fuzzy need to an executed plan: business, product and talent management.

groups.title: AI with judgment
groups.desc: Brought into your processes only when needed. Always with human review.

std.headline: I don't ship a line I don't understand.
std.body: These are the principles I won't negotiate. They're why a founder can sleep easy with what goes to production, and why a dev team gets better working with me instead of piling up debt.

items.d: The data model isn't improvised: it's justified.
Every task is documented and estimated before touching the keyboard.
Working isn't enough; once it works, it gets cleaned up.
I check for gaps before releasing, not after the incident.

blogHome.headline: What I think, written down.
blogHome.sub: Essays and notes on engineering, product leadership and using AI without handing over the thinking.

modes.headline: Three ways to help. One conversation.
modes.sub: Depending on what you need, I step in as one of the three. Sometimes as all three.

items.title: Developer
items.body: Strong technical skills in architecture and standards, always caring for UX and UI. Clean code your team can maintain when I'm not around.

items.title: Technical advisor
items.body: I diagnose and improve your software development processes with business sense. Technical structure paired with your operation's judgment. I bring AI into your flow with criteria — only when it truly adds value.

items.title: Product & project director
items.body: Product, planning and execution: discovery, specification, Scrum and project management. I manage team and talent so business and development move together.

traj.headline: A decade of building, failing and building again.

items.d: An e-learning platform for students. First company; first hard lesson.
Development for SMBs across many industries. I learned that growing without structure kills faster than not growing.
A credit and prospect-evaluation system. Solid data model and financial architecture.
I lead software development and product design for membership software, with active clients.

items.q: Do you code or just lead?
items.a: Both. I diagnose, I lead and, when it adds value, I implement it myself.

items.q: Which companies do you work with?
items.a: Mexican SMBs that already have software and need it to pay off. I also work remotely.

items.q: Do you have a full-time role besides consulting?
items.a: Yes. My main role is in product and development leadership; independent consulting is compatible and disclosed — it doesn't compete with it.
An accelerator with judgment. Any AI-generated code is reviewed, understood and justified before delivery. It never replaces analysis.
Thirty minutes over video. No proposal or commitment behind it. To understand what you have today and what you'd need tomorrow.

ctaFinal.headline: Let's start with a conversation.
ctaFinal.sub: 30 minutes. No proposal or contract behind it. A call to understand what you have today and what you'd need tomorrow.

404.body: The link is broken or the page moved. Let's get back to solid ground.
